module.label: Pediatric headache, seizure, altered mental status, or focal neurologic concern

use_when: Child or adolescent with headache, first or recurrent seizure, blackout with seizure markers, altered conscious state, persistent post-ictal state, focal neurologic symptoms, papilledema concern, raised intracranial pressure concern, or neurologic symptoms after head injury.

Pediatric neuro workup needs age-band routing, baseline neurologic status, bedside glucose, seizure/status escalation, headache red flags, infection or head-injury routing, focused neurologic maneuvers, and imaging/EEG stewardship.
do_not_use_when: Adult patient; use the adult stroke, TIA, seizure, headache, or altered mental status pathway as appropriate.
Unstable child with active seizure, airway compromise, shock, severe trauma, suspected sepsis, or rapidly falling conscious state; escalate immediately while documenting focused findings.
Primary syncope/chest pain without seizure markers or neurologic symptoms; use the pediatric chest pain/syncope/palpitations pathway.
Primary fever without headache, seizure, altered mental status, meningism, or focal neurologic symptoms; use the pediatric fever/sepsis pathway.
Known specialty neurology, neurosurgery, oncology, transplant, metabolic, or epilepsy protocol where a local specialty pathway overrides this general pediatric emergency neuro module.

modifier_add_on_policy.rationale: This validated pediatric neuro pack already includes fever, meningitis, encephalitis, sepsis, rash, travel, and infection escalation; generic infection-source prompts should not override the neuro-specific source-localizing history.

requiredQuestions.label: Ask age and neurodevelopment baseline
requiredQuestions.text: What is the child's exact age, neurodevelopmental baseline, communication baseline, seizure history, disability baseline, and usual mobility or behavior?
requiredQuestions.when_to_ask: Ask first for every pediatric neuro concern.
requiredQuestions.diagnostic_purpose: Age and baseline change headache referral thresholds, seizure risk, GCS interpretation, behavior change interpretation, and discharge safety.
requiredQuestions.management_implication: Positive infant, under-4 headache, developmental-delay, disability, or changed-baseline context changes senior review, testing, admission, follow-up, and adult-pathway exclusion.
requiredQuestions.options: Multi-select: under 6 months / 6 to 23 months / 2 to 3 years / 4 to 11 years / 12 years or older / known developmental difference / known epilepsy / baseline changed / baseline unchanged
requiredQuestions.likelihood_ratio_note: Question-level LR+/LR- is not available; age band and baseline are source-defined routing variables.

source.implementation_notes: Do not use adult mental-status assumptions for children or children with disability.

requiredQuestions.label: Ask headache pattern
requiredQuestions.text: For headache, when did it start, where is it located, how severe is it, is it acute recurrent or chronic/progressive, and what triggers or relieves it?
requiredQuestions.when_to_ask: Ask when headache, vomiting with headache, visual symptoms, or caregiver concern for headache is present.
requiredQuestions.diagnostic_purpose: Distinguishes primary headache patterns from chronic progressive, sudden severe, positional, occipital, or sleep-related secondary headache patterns.
requiredQuestions.management_implication: Progressive, positional, sudden severe, occipital, morning, sleep-waking, or activity-limiting patterns change senior review, imaging discussion, follow-up, and discharge safety.
requiredQuestions.options: Multi-select: sudden severe / progressive worsening / early morning / wakes from sleep / cough sneeze or bend trigger / positional / occipital / recurrent nonprogressive / activity-limiting / none of these / unable to obtain
requiredQuestions.likelihood_ratio_note: Question-level LR+/LR- is not available; RCH/NICE red-flag patterns change urgency rather than providing one LR.

source.implementation_notes: Keep pattern history atomic and multi-select rather than a generic headache HPI paragraph.

requiredQuestions.label: Ask headache-associated danger features
requiredQuestions.text: With headache, are vomiting without another clear cause, ataxia, squint or upward-gaze difficulty, visual change, papilledema concern, confusion, lethargy, focal neurologic symptoms, seizure, fever with meningism, recent head injury, shunt, malignancy, immunosuppression, hypertension, bleeding tendency, or anticoagulant/antiplatelet use present?
requiredQuestions.when_to_ask: Ask in every pediatric headache or suspected raised-ICP presentation.
requiredQuestions.diagnostic_purpose: Identifies source-defined same-day assessment, urgent referral, imaging, infection, head-injury, and specialty-risk features.
requiredQuestions.management_implication: Any positive feature changes senior review, imaging/LP safety discussion, infection/head-injury pathway, admission/transfer threshold, and discharge constraints.
requiredQuestions.options: Multi-select: vomiting / ataxia / abnormal eye movement or squint / visual change / papilledema concern / confusion or lethargy / focal neurologic symptom / seizure / fever with neck stiffness or photophobia / recent head injury / VP shunt / malignancy or immunosuppression / hypertension / bleeding tendency or anticoagulant / none / unable to obtain
requiredQuestions.likelihood_ratio_note: Question-level LR+/LR- is not available for this red-flag cluster; each positive feature changes management by guideline rule.

source.source_section: headaches in children under 12 and recurrent headache recommendations
source.implementation_notes: Under-12 same-day criteria and under-4 urgent neuro assessment rules must remain visible.

requiredQuestions.label: Ask seizure or blackout event details
requiredQuestions.text: For seizure, blackout, or altered event, how did it start, how long did it last, was it focal or generalized, was there full recovery between events, were rescue medicines given, and is the child still seizing?
requiredQuestions.when_to_ask: Ask for seizure, blackout with seizure markers, vacant spells, altered event, or unexplained collapse.
requiredQuestions.diagnostic_purpose: Distinguishes status epilepticus, first seizure, focal seizure, complex febrile seizure, syncope mimic, and recurrent epilepsy.
requiredQuestions.management_implication: Ongoing seizure, duration over 5 minutes, repeated events without recovery, focal onset, rescue medicines, or incomplete recovery changes emergency medication, glucose/labs, imaging, admission, transfer, and follow-up.
requiredQuestions.options: Multi-select: still seizing / lasted over 5 minutes / repeated without recovery / focal onset or focal signs / generalized convulsion / vacant spell or altered awareness / rescue medication given / first seizure / known epilepsy typical event / full recovery / no seizure markers / unable to obtain
requiredQuestions.likelihood_ratio_note: Question-level LR+/LR- is not applicable to status epilepticus; duration and recovery define emergency management.

source.implementation_notes: Active seizure should bypass routine workflow and trigger emergency protocol.

requiredQuestions.label: Ask recovery and focal deficit status
requiredQuestions.text: After the event, has the child returned to their usual baseline, and are persistent weakness, facial droop, aphasia, vision change, diplopia, ataxia, severe headache, or behavior change present?
requiredQuestions.when_to_ask: Ask after seizure, blackout, head injury, altered consciousness, or any focal neurologic concern.
requiredQuestions.diagnostic_purpose: Identifies post-ictal recovery, persistent focal signs, pediatric stroke concern, raised ICP, infection, trauma, or metabolic/toxic causes.
requiredQuestions.management_implication: Incomplete recovery or persistent focal findings change glucose/labs, imaging, EEG/referral, senior review, admission, transfer, and discharge safety.
requiredQuestions.options: Multi-select: returned to baseline / not at baseline / persistent weakness / facial droop / aphasia or speech change / vision change or diplopia / ataxia / severe headache / behavior change / none of these / unable to assess
requiredQuestions.likelihood_ratio_note: Question-level LR+/LR- is not available; persistent deficits are management-changing red flags.

source.implementation_notes: Persistent focal findings after the post-ictal phase should not be hidden under a generic seizure label.

requiredQuestions.label: Ask fever meningitis encephalitis features
requiredQuestions.text: Are fever, neck stiffness, photophobia, rash or petechiae, severe irritability, persistent vomiting, immunization gap, sick contacts, travel, recent antibiotics, or concern for meningitis/encephalitis/sepsis present?
requiredQuestions.when_to_ask: Ask when headache, seizure, altered consciousness, fever, rash, or systemic illness is present.
requiredQuestions.diagnostic_purpose: Identifies meningitis, encephalitis, sepsis, intracranial abscess, febrile seizure context, and infection-source routing.
requiredQuestions.management_implication: Positive features change isolation, cultures/labs, antibiotics/antivirals, LP safety, imaging, senior review, admission, and transfer.
requiredQuestions.options: Multi-select: fever / neck stiffness / photophobia / petechiae or purpura / severe irritability / persistent vomiting / immunization gap / sick contacts / travel / recent antibiotics / none of these / unable to obtain
requiredQuestions.likelihood_ratio_note: Question-level LR+/LR- is not available for this infection cluster; source features trigger infection and sepsis pathways.

source.implementation_notes: Do not perform LP in a child with reduced GCS; stabilize and follow local infection pathway.

requiredQuestions.label: Ask trauma toxin metabolic context
requiredQuestions.text: Has there been head injury, fall, possible non-accidental injury, poisoning or drug exposure, alcohol or substance use, diabetes, poor intake, dehydration, renal/hepatic disease, hypothermia, inborn-error/metabolic condition, or medication overdose possibility?
requiredQuestions.when_to_ask: Ask for altered consciousness, seizure, severe headache, unexplained behavior change, or neurologic deficit.
requiredQuestions.diagnostic_purpose: Identifies traumatic, safeguarding, toxicologic, hypoglycemic, DKA/electrolyte, hepatic/renal, and metabolic causes.
requiredQuestions.management_implication: Positive features change glucose/labs/toxicology, head injury imaging, safeguarding review, poison center contact, DKA/metabolic workup, senior review, admission, and transfer.
requiredQuestions.options: Multi-select: head injury or fall / possible non-accidental injury / poisoning or medication exposure / alcohol or substance use / diabetes / poor intake or dehydration / renal or hepatic disease / hypothermia / known metabolic condition / overdose possible / none of these / unable to obtain confidentially
requiredQuestions.likelihood_ratio_note: Question-level LR+/LR- is not available; these are source-defined causes that redirect management.

source.implementation_notes: Use confidential adolescent history and local safeguarding policy where relevant.

conditionalQuestions.label: Ask under-4 head size and raised-ICP context
conditionalQuestions.text: For a child under 4 years or concern for abnormal head shape/size, has head circumference changed across centile lines, or are tense fontanelle, vomiting, unsteadiness, sixth-nerve palsy, upward-gaze failure, or headache present?
conditionalQuestions.options: Multi-select: head circumference crossing centiles / tense fontanelle / vomiting / unsteadiness or ataxia / sixth nerve palsy / upward-gaze failure / headache / none / not applicable / unable to measure
conditionalQuestions.when_to_ask: Ask when the child is under 4 years, has abnormal head shape/size concern, or has raised-ICP features.
conditionalQuestions.diagnostic_purpose: Identifies NICE urgent pediatric/neuro assessment and imaging triggers in younger children.
conditionalQuestions.management_implication: Positive features change urgent pediatric assessment, cranial imaging, senior review, admission, transfer, and discharge safety.

source.source_section: headaches under 4 and head shape or size abnormalities
source.implementation_notes: Do not apply adult headache reassurance to children under 4 with headache.

conditionalQuestions.likelihood_ratio_note: Question-level LR+/LR- is not available; NICE uses these findings as referral/imaging triggers.

conditionalQuestions.label: Ask known epilepsy and rescue plan
conditionalQuestions.text: For known epilepsy or recurrent seizures, is this a typical event, is there a seizure management plan, have rescue medicines been used, are medicines missed, has sleep illness or trigger changed, and are seizures more frequent or uncontrolled?
conditionalQuestions.options: Multi-select: typical event / atypical event / seizure plan available / rescue medication used / missed antiseizure medicine / illness or sleep trigger / more frequent or uncontrolled / no known epilepsy / unable to obtain
conditionalQuestions.when_to_ask: Ask when known epilepsy, recurrent seizure, rescue medicine, or seizure plan is mentioned.
conditionalQuestions.diagnostic_purpose: Distinguishes typical breakthrough seizure from uncontrolled epilepsy, medication issue, acute illness, or atypical event requiring review.
conditionalQuestions.management_implication: Atypical, frequent, uncontrolled, no plan, or incomplete recovery changes medication review, labs, EEG/neuro follow-up, admission, and discharge education.

source.implementation_notes: Avoid CT in established epilepsy after a typical seizure unless other indications are present.

conditionalQuestions.likelihood_ratio_note: Question-level LR+/LR- is not available; event typicality and recovery change imaging and consultation.

conditionalQuestions.label: Ask adolescent confidential neuro context
conditionalQuestions.text: For an adolescent, are pregnancy possible, postpartum state, estrogen therapy, substance use, eating disorder, migraine triggers, bullying/stress, medication overuse, self-harm, or unsafe environment relevant?
conditionalQuestions.options: Multi-select: pregnancy possible / postpartum / estrogen therapy / substance use / eating disorder / stress or bullying / medication overuse / self-harm concern / unsafe environment / none endorsed / unable to obtain confidentially
conditionalQuestions.when_to_ask: Ask confidentially for adolescents with headache, seizure, altered mental status, syncope mimic, or focal neurologic concern.
conditionalQuestions.diagnostic_purpose: Identifies pregnancy/medication/imaging constraints, substance/toxin causes, medication-overuse headache, psychosocial triggers, and safety risks.
conditionalQuestions.management_implication: Positive context changes pregnancy testing, medication choice, toxin workup, mental-health/safeguarding review, follow-up, and specialty routing.

source.source_section: other features and adolescent psychosocial factors
source.implementation_notes: Use local confidential adolescent-care policy and avoid PHI-rich export.

conditionalQuestions.likelihood_ratio_note: Question-level LR+/LR- is not applicable to confidential context; positive context changes safety and medication choices.

conditionalQuestions.label: Ask shunt bleeding immune-risk context
conditionalQuestions.text: Is there a VP shunt, neurosurgery, malignancy, immunosuppression, bleeding disorder, anticoagulant/antiplatelet use, prothrombotic condition, sickle cell disease, or severe anemia?
conditionalQuestions.options: Multi-select: VP shunt / recent neurosurgery / malignancy / immunosuppression / bleeding disorder / anticoagulant or antiplatelet / prothrombotic condition / sickle cell disease / severe anemia / none / unable to obtain
conditionalQuestions.when_to_ask: Ask when headache, seizure, focal deficit, altered mental status, trauma, or systemic disease context is present.
conditionalQuestions.diagnostic_purpose: Identifies higher-risk intracranial, infectious, hemorrhagic, thrombotic, or hematologic pathways.
conditionalQuestions.management_implication: Positive context changes imaging, cultures/labs, specialty consultation, anticoagulation safety, transfusion/hematology review, admission, and transfer.

source.source_section: red flags: systemic disorder, medications, and VP shunt
source.implementation_notes: These risk contexts should appear as limitations and escalation triggers, not hidden metadata.

conditionalQuestions.likelihood_ratio_note: Question-level LR+/LR- is not available; these contexts change threshold for urgent workup.

requiredExam.label: Inspect pupils
requiredExam.technique: Inspect pupil size, symmetry, and reactivity to light.
requiredExam.findings_options: Equal reactive / unequal / fixed or poorly reactive / dilated / pinpoint / unable to assess
requiredExam.when_to_perform: Perform in every pediatric headache, seizure, altered mental status, or focal neurologic concern when safe.
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: Raised intracranial pressure, herniation risk, third nerve palsy, toxin effect, or focal neurologic lesion.
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available; abnormal pupils are source-backed raised-ICP or toxin/focal-neuro clues.
requiredExam.management_change: Abnormal pupils change urgent senior review, imaging, airway/resuscitation, toxin evaluation, and transfer threshold.
requiredExam.limitations: Ambient light, medications, trauma, and cooperation affect assessment.

source.source_section: examination: signs of raised intracranial pressure
source.implementation_notes: This is an individual bedside maneuver, not a generic neurologic exam bundle.

requiredExam.label: Inspect eye movements
requiredExam.technique: Ask the child to follow a target horizontally and vertically when developmentally able; observe for squint, sixth-nerve palsy, diplopia, nystagmus, or upward-gaze limitation.
requiredExam.findings_options: normal / squint / sixth nerve palsy concern / diplopia / nystagmus / upward-gaze limitation / unable to assess
requiredExam.when_to_perform: Perform when headache, visual symptoms, raised-ICP concern, focal symptoms, or altered mental status is present.
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: Raised intracranial pressure, cranial neuropathy, brainstem/cerebellar process, migraine aura, or focal lesion.
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available; abnormal eye movement findings are guideline-defined red flags.
requiredExam.management_change: Abnormal findings change urgent imaging/senior review, neurology or ophthalmology discussion, and discharge safety.
requiredExam.limitations: Age, development, cooperation, and visual acuity affect interpretation.

source.source_section: headache and raised intracranial pressure referral features
source.implementation_notes: Failure of upward gaze or squint with headache is not a benign migraine feature.

requiredExam.label: Test limb strength
requiredExam.technique: Test age-appropriate antigravity and resisted strength in each limb, including pronator drift when able.
requiredExam.findings_options: symmetric strength / unilateral weakness / bilateral weakness / pronator drift / posturing / unable to assess
requiredExam.when_to_perform: Perform when focal symptoms, seizure recovery, altered mental status, headache red flags, or stroke concern is present.
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: Persistent focal neurologic deficit, stroke, intracranial lesion, Todd paresis, spinal process, or generalized weakness.
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available; persistent focal weakness changes management regardless of adult scoring.
requiredExam.management_change: Abnormal strength changes glucose/metabolic correction, imaging, neurology/senior review, admission, and transfer threshold.
requiredExam.limitations: Pain, developmental stage, cooperation, post-ictal state, and baseline disability affect interpretation.

source.implementation_notes: Document whether weakness persists after expected post-ictal recovery.

requiredExam.label: Test coordination or gait when safe
requiredExam.technique: When safe and developmentally appropriate, observe sitting balance, reaching, finger-nose testing, tandem or normal gait, and truncal stability.
requiredExam.findings_options: normal / ataxia / wide-based gait / truncal instability / unable or unsafe to test / baseline differs
requiredExam.when_to_perform: Perform when headache, dizziness/vertigo, focal symptoms, seizure recovery, or altered mental status is present and the child can be tested safely.
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: Ataxia, cerebellar process, raised intracranial pressure, intoxication, posterior circulation process, or gait-threatening neurologic disease.
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available; ataxia is a source-defined red flag in pediatric headache.
requiredExam.management_change: Ataxia or unsafe gait changes senior review, imaging, toxin/metabolic workup, admission, transfer, and discharge safety.
requiredExam.limitations: Do not force unsafe gait testing; fatigue, medications, baseline disability, and developmental stage affect interpretation.

source.implementation_notes: Gait/coordination is an individual maneuver, not a generic broad neuro exam label.

requiredExam.label: Inspect optic discs by fundoscopy
requiredExam.technique: Perform or request fundoscopy to assess for papilledema when recurrent headache, red flags, visual symptoms, raised-ICP concern, or local capability allows.
requiredExam.findings_options: normal disc / papilledema concern / unable to visualize / deferred to clinician with equipment / not feasible
requiredExam.when_to_perform: Perform or request in recurrent headache and any raised-ICP concern; document if not feasible.
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: Papilledema, raised intracranial pressure, optic nerve abnormality, or urgent neuroimaging trigger.
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available; NICE and RCH treat papilledema/abnormal fundoscopy as urgent.
requiredExam.management_change: Papilledema or inability to assess with red flags changes urgent senior review, neuroimaging, ophthalmology/neurology route, and discharge safety.
requiredExam.limitations: Bedside fundoscopy can be difficult; a failed exam does not rule out papilledema.

source.implementation_notes: Expose fundoscopy as a specific maneuver, not hidden inside broad neuro exam.

requiredExam.label: Measure temperature
requiredExam.action: Measure and document current temperature with age-appropriate method.
requiredExam.management_change: Fever changes need for infection-source review, cultures/labs, antibiotics/antivirals, lumbar-puncture safety assessment, admission, and transfer.
requiredExam.options: ___ C/F / Febrile / Afebrile / Hypothermic / Not available
requiredExam.limitations: Antipyretics and timing can mask fever trajectory.
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: LR is not applicable to temperature as baseline clinical data.

source.implementation_notes: Keep temperature in baseline data, not physical exam maneuvers.

requiredExam.technique: Measure temperature using an appropriate bedside thermometer route.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: Perform when Pediatric headache, seizure, altered mental status, or focal neurologic concern is the selected validated workup and the maneuver is clinically safe and relevant at bedside.
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: Fever changes headache, seizure, or altered mental status toward meningitis, encephalitis, sepsis, or febrile seizure pathways.

requiredExam.label: Measure heart rate
requiredExam.action: Measure and document heart rate with age-band interpretation and rhythm impression.
requiredExam.management_change: Abnormal heart rate changes monitoring, ECG, resuscitation, poison/metabolic workup, senior review, and transfer threshold.
requiredExam.options: ___ bpm / age-appropriate / tachycardic / bradycardic / irregular / not available
requiredExam.limitations: Pain, fever, crying, medications, and post-ictal state affect interpretation.
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: LR is not applicable to heart rate as baseline safety data.

source.source_section: examination: cardiac and raised intracranial pressure signs
source.implementation_notes: Bradycardia with hypertension is a late raised-ICP sign and should trigger urgent escalation.

requiredExam.technique: Palpate or monitor heart rate for a full interval, noting rate and regularity.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: Perform when Pediatric headache, seizure, altered mental status, or focal neurologic concern is the selected validated workup and the maneuver is clinically safe and relevant at bedside.
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: Tachycardia, bradycardia, or irregular rhythm can signal sepsis, shock, raised intracranial pressure, toxin, arrhythmia mimic, or post-seizure instability.

requiredExam.label: Measure respiratory rate
requiredExam.action: Measure respiratory rate and respiratory pattern.
requiredExam.management_change: Abnormal respiratory status changes airway support, oxygen, blood gas, senior review, ICU or transfer threshold, and medication timing.
requiredExam.options: ___ /min / age-appropriate / tachypnea / bradypnea / irregular / apnea concern / not available
requiredExam.limitations: Crying, sedation, seizure activity, and post-ictal state affect interpretation.
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: LR is not applicable to respiratory rate as baseline safety data.

source.implementation_notes: Airway and breathing concerns take priority over checklist completion.

requiredExam.technique: Count respiratory rate over a full interval, noting rate and effort.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: Perform when Pediatric headache, seizure, altered mental status, or focal neurologic concern is the selected validated workup and the maneuver is clinically safe and relevant at bedside.
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: Respiratory depression, irregular respirations, hypoxia, or shock can drive altered mental status and determine seizure medication safety.

requiredExam.label: Measure blood pressure
requiredExam.action: Measure blood pressure and interpret against pediatric age/height context when available.
requiredExam.management_change: Abnormal BP changes resuscitation, raised-ICP concern, hypertensive emergency workup, medication/toxin review, and disposition.
requiredExam.options: ___/___ mmHg / age-appropriate / hypertensive / hypotensive / orthostatic concern / not available
requiredExam.limitations: Incorrect cuff size and agitation can distort measurements; repeat abnormal values.
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: LR is not applicable to blood pressure as baseline safety data.

source.source_section: recurrent headaches and blood pressure recommendation
source.implementation_notes: Headache workups need BP with pediatric interpretation rather than adult-only thresholds.

requiredExam.technique: Measure blood pressure using an appropriately sized cuff after a brief rest period, seated or supine as clinically appropriate.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: Perform when Pediatric headache, seizure, altered mental status, or focal neurologic concern is the selected validated workup and the maneuver is clinically safe and relevant at bedside.
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: Hypertension can cause headache and can be a raised-ICP clue when paired with bradycardia; hypotension suggests shock or sepsis.

requiredExam.label: Measure oxygen saturation
requiredExam.action: Measure oxygen saturation and document oxygen delivery or room air.
requiredExam.management_change: Low saturation triggers oxygen, airway/breathing reassessment, respiratory source review, senior review, and transfer threshold.
requiredExam.options: ___% room air / ___% on oxygen / low / normal / not available
requiredExam.limitations: Motion, poor perfusion, nail products, and seizure activity can impair readings.
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: LR is not applicable to oxygen saturation as baseline safety data.

source.implementation_notes: Oxygen saturation is baseline safety data, not a physical exam maneuver.

requiredExam.technique: Measure oxygen saturation by pulse oximetry.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: Perform when Pediatric headache, seizure, altered mental status, or focal neurologic concern is the selected validated workup and the maneuver is clinically safe and relevant at bedside.
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: Hypoxemia can cause altered mental status, worsen seizures, and change airway/transfer decisions.

requiredExam.label: Determine mental status with AVPU or pediatric GCS
requiredExam.action: Document AVPU and formal pediatric GCS when any impairment or caregiver-reported altered baseline is present.
requiredExam.management_change: Abnormal or falling score changes resuscitation priority, imaging, LP safety, antibiotics/antivirals, senior review, admission, and transfer.
requiredExam.options: Alert / responds to voice / responds to pain / unresponsive / GCS ___ / baseline differs / not available
requiredExam.findings_options: AVPU alert / AVPU responds to voice / AVPU responds to pain / AVPU unresponsive / pediatric GCS ___ / caregiver says baseline differs / not available
requiredExam.limitations: Developmental delay, sedation, post-ictal state, intoxication, and language barriers affect scoring.
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: LR is not applicable to conscious-state scoring as baseline safety data.

source.implementation_notes: Parents are often the best judge of altered baseline, especially for children with disability.

requiredExam.technique: Determine mental status using AVPU or a pediatric Glasgow Coma Scale.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: Perform when Pediatric headache, seizure, altered mental status, or focal neurologic concern is the selected validated workup and the maneuver is clinically safe and relevant at bedside.
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: RCH defines altered conscious state with GCS below 15 and notes any AVPU impairment should prompt formal GCS.

requiredExam.label: Measure bedside glucose
requiredExam.action: Check point-of-care glucose for seizure, altered mental status, weakness, suspected metabolic cause, diabetes, poor intake, or unexplained neuro symptoms.
requiredExam.management_change: Abnormal glucose changes immediate treatment, DKA/metabolic workup, seizure management, admission, and safe discharge.
requiredExam.options: ___ mg/dL or mmol/L / low / high / normal / not available
requiredExam.limitations: Confirm unexpected values per local policy without delaying treatment of severe hypoglycemia.
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: LR is not applicable to bedside glucose as baseline safety data; abnormal results directly change management.

source.implementation_notes: All actively seizing or post-seizure children need rapid glucose availability.

requiredExam.technique: Measure bedside glucose using point-of-care testing.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: Perform when Pediatric headache, seizure, altered mental status, or focal neurologic concern is the selected validated workup and the maneuver is clinically safe and relevant at bedside.
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: RCH and NICE identify hypoglycemia/metabolic disturbance as a seizure and altered-conscious-state mimic that must be corrected promptly.

requiredExam.label: Measure current weight
requiredExam.action: Document current measured or best estimated weight in kg.
requiredExam.management_change: Missing weight blocks safe dosing for benzodiazepines, second-line antiseizure agents, fluids, analgesia, antimicrobials, and sedation planning.
requiredExam.options: ___ kg measured / ___ kg estimated / Broselow or local estimate / not available
requiredExam.limitations: Use actual weight when possible and document if estimated during resuscitation.
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: LR is not applicable to weight as baseline dosing data.

source.implementation_notes: Weight is baseline safety data, not physical exam.

requiredExam.technique: Measure current weight using a calibrated scale.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: Perform when Pediatric headache, seizure, altered mental status, or focal neurologic concern is the selected validated workup and the maneuver is clinically safe and relevant at bedside.
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: Pediatric seizure medicines, fluids, analgesia, antimicrobials, and transfer handoffs are weight-based.

conditionalExam.label: Test neck stiffness
conditionalExam.technique: Assess for neck stiffness or meningism in an age-appropriate way without forcing painful movement in an unstable child.
conditionalExam.findings_options: absent / present / photophobia present / not tolerated / unsafe to assess
conditionalExam.when_to_perform: Perform when fever, headache, photophobia, rash, altered mental status, or meningitis/encephalitis concern is present.
conditionalExam.diagnostic_target: Meningitis, encephalitis, sepsis, raised intracranial pressure, or other CNS infection.
conditionalExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available; meningism changes infection and LP-safety pathway.
conditionalExam.management_change: Positive or concerning findings change cultures/labs, antibiotics/antivirals, LP safety, imaging, admission, and transfer.
conditionalExam.limitations: Young age, irritability, analgesia, and altered mental status can limit reliability.

source.source_section: red flag findings: signs of meningism
source.implementation_notes: Do not delay antibiotics/antivirals if suspected CNS infection and the child is unwell.

conditionalExam.label: Inspect skin for rash or bruising
conditionalExam.technique: Inspect exposed skin for petechiae, purpura, bruising, birth marks, sweating, needle marks, or neurocutaneous stigmata.
conditionalExam.findings_options: none / petechiae or purpura / bruising / neurocutaneous stigmata / sweating or needle marks / unable to assess
conditionalExam.when_to_perform: Perform when fever, altered mental status, seizure, headache red flags, bruising, toxin, or safeguarding concern is present.
conditionalExam.diagnostic_target: Sepsis/meningococcemia, bleeding disorder, non-accidental injury, toxin exposure, neurocutaneous syndrome, or systemic disease.
conditionalExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available; positive findings change management by source-defined risk.
conditionalExam.management_change: Positive findings change sepsis/rash pathway, cultures/labs, safeguarding review, hematology workup, toxicology review, and disposition.
conditionalExam.limitations: Respect privacy and document if full skin inspection is deferred.

source.implementation_notes: Route prominent rash to the pediatric rash/skin module.

conditionalExam.label: Inspect head for trauma signs
conditionalExam.technique: Inspect scalp, face, ears, and nose for hematoma, bruising, CSF-like fluid, blood, mastoid signs, or concerning injury pattern.
conditionalExam.findings_options: none / scalp hematoma / bruising / ear or nasal blood or clear fluid / mastoid signs / injury pattern concern / unable to assess
conditionalExam.when_to_perform: Perform when head injury, seizure or amnesia after injury, vomiting, altered mental status, or safeguarding concern is present.
conditionalExam.diagnostic_target: Head injury, skull fracture, intracranial injury, CSF leak, non-accidental injury, or infection source.
conditionalExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available here; head injury findings route to local head injury imaging and observation rules.
conditionalExam.management_change: Positive findings change head injury pathway, imaging, safeguarding review, admission, observation, and transfer.
conditionalExam.limitations: A normal external exam does not exclude intracranial injury.

source.implementation_notes: Use local head injury and child-safety pathways when positive.

conditionalExam.label: Measure head circumference
conditionalExam.technique: For children under 4 years with abnormal head size/shape or raised-ICP concern, take 3 head-circumference measurements and plot the longest on an appropriate chart.
conditionalExam.findings_options: normal trend / below 2nd centile / crosses 2 or more centile lines / rapidly increasing / unable to measure / not applicable
conditionalExam.when_to_perform: Perform for children under 4 with suspected abnormal head shape/size or raised-ICP concern.
conditionalExam.diagnostic_target: Hydrocephalus, microcephaly, progressive raised intracranial pressure, or abnormal head growth.
conditionalExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available; NICE defines centile changes as referral/imaging triggers.
conditionalExam.management_change: Abnormal measurement changes urgent pediatric assessment, cranial imaging, follow-up, admission, and transfer.
conditionalExam.limitations: Measurement technique and chart selection affect interpretation.

source.source_section: head shape or size abnormalities under 4 years
source.implementation_notes: This is an individual measurement, not a generic pediatric exam.

clinical_cutoff_review.review_note: Cutoff rows were added because the prior module evidence did not expose enough explicit guideline thresholds for pathway traversal.
